tests.name: OBBB Senior Deduction: $6,000 per person
tests.source: OBBB Act 2025 §102; IRS FS-2025-03; signed July 4, 2025 as P.L. 119-21

tests.name: OBBB phaseout rate: 6% ($60 per $1,000 over threshold)
tests.source: OBBB Act 2025 §102; phases out completely at $175K single / $250K MFJ

tests.name: Below base threshold: $20K SS, $10K other → 0% taxable
At base threshold: $24K SS, $20K other → 0% taxable
Above upper: $30K SS, $60K other → 85% rule
tests.source: IRS Pub 915, Worksheet 1; CI=$30K*0.5+$60K=$75K; lower=min($15K, $6K)=$6K; taxable=min($25.5K, ($75K-$44K)*0.85+$6K)=$32.35K→capped at $25.5K

tests.name: Very high income: $40K SS, $200K other → 85% cap
Income above wage cap ($200K) capped at $176,100
Claim at FRA (67) → 100% of PIA
tests.source: SSA basic rule: no adjustment at FRA

tests.name: Claim at 62 (60 months early) → 70% of PIA
Claim at 64 (36 months early) → 80% of PIA
Claim at 65 (24 months early) → ~86.7% of PIA
Claim at 70 (36 months late) → 124% of PIA
Claim at 68 (12 months late) → 108% of PIA
Claim at 72 → same as 70 (DRC capped)
tests.source: SSA: no DRC credits after age 70

Tax computed for retirement year

tests.source: Engine logic: SS begins at ssClaimAge

tests.name: Account balances sum to net worth
Higher income → higher lifetime tax
tests.source: Progressive tax: more income = more tax

tests.name: Roth conversion increases tax in conversion year
tests.source: Conversions are taxable as ordinary income (IRS)

tests.name: Roth-first order depletes Roth faster
tests.source: Engine: Roth-first spends Roth before other accounts

tests.name: California adds state tax vs no-state
tests.source: CA 9.3% effective rate increases total tax

calcTax on negative income → $0

tests.name: IRMAA at exact $212K MFJ threshold → Tier 1
tests.source: CMS: MAGI ≥ threshold triggers next tier

tests.name: Under FRA: $100K earnings, $16,800 benefit → capped at $16,800
tests.source: SSA Pub 05-10069: withholding cannot exceed benefit

tests.name: At FRA (age 67, born 1960+): $200K earnings → $0
tests.source: SSA basic rule: no test at/after FRA

tests.name: After FRA (age 70): $200K earnings → $0
tests.source: SSA basic rule: no test at/after FRA

tests.name: Zero earnings → $0
tests.source: SSA basic rule: no excess earnings

tests.name: SSDI: exempt from earnings test
tests.source: SSA: SSDI uses different SGA rules

tests.source: Engine: early claimer with earnings has ssWithheld > 0

tests.name: Integration: age 67 (FRA, retired) no withholding

tests.name: 200% FPL enhanced (2025) → 2% applicable
tests.source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-35: 2% applicable at 200% FPL

tests.source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-35: 6% applicable at 300% FPL

tests.name: 400% FPL enhanced (2025) → 8.5% applicable
tests.source: IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-35: 8.5% applicable at 400% FPL

tests.name: Below 100% FPL → not eligible for subsidy
400%+ FPL original (2026) → no subsidy (cliff)
tests.source: ACA original: no PTC above 400% FPL

tests.name: Full subsidy at 150% FPL enhanced = benchmark premium
tests.source: ACA: 0% applicable → subsidy covers full benchmark

tests.name: Term: no benefit if insured outlives term (death 80, expires 75)
Term: benefit pays when death before expiry (death 72, expires 75)
Permanent: premiums active before death (age 84)
tests.source: Engine: permanent type, not dead → premium collected

tests.name: Permanent: benefit pays at death age 85
tests.source: Engine: permanent type, justDied → pays deathBenefit

tests.name: Death benefit does NOT inflate MAGI by benefit amount (IRC §101)
tests.source: IRC §101: $500K benefit not in gross income. MAGI diff from withdrawal mix only.

tests.name: No benefit when no death age configured
tests.source: Engine: deathAge is null → justDied never true

tests.name: Multiple policies: both pay out at death
tests.source: Engine: loop over all policies, each eligible one pays

tests.name: Spouse policy: benefit triggers at spouse death
HSA contribution reduces federal taxable income
HSA contribution does NOT reduce CA state income

tests.name: HSA contribution DOES reduce OR state income
HSA balance grows at configured rate tax-free
HSA medical withdrawal at age 60: tax-free

tests.source: Non-qualified pre-65 HSA withdrawal: ordinary income + 20% penalty

tests.source: Qualified medical HSA withdrawals are tax-free post-65

tests.source: Medical HSA withdrawals do not count toward MAGI

tests.source: Non-medical portion post-65 treated as ordinary income

tests.source: HSAs have no Required Minimum Distributions
